content: юА│is phenomenon, however, doesnтАЩt come close to the complete division between full wakefulness and truly deep NREM sleep achieved by each side of birdsтАЩ and dolphinsтАЩ brains. Humans always have to sleep with both halves of our brain in some state of NREM sleep. Imagine, though, the possibilities that would become available if only we could rest our brains, one half at a time.
